1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"sync/atomic"
7		"testing"
8	
9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
11		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
12		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
13	)
14	
15	// pinPropagationSpec mirrors the worker-level pins captured by the executor
16	// closures in cmd/agent_cmd.go (CLI dispatch) and internal/server/workers.go
17	// (server dispatch). Production code captures these into the closure body and
18	// reads them on every invocation; this struct lets a regression test simulate
19	// the same wiring and verify per-iteration values.
20	type pinPropagationSpec struct {
21		Harness  string
22		Model    string
23		Profile  string
24		Provider string
25	}
26	
27	type capturedRequest struct {
28		pinPropagationSpec
29		BeadID string
30	}
31	
32	// pinCapturingExecutor models the production executor closures: it records
33	// the worker-level pins observed on every invocation. Production closures
34	// build a service.Execute / RouteRequest from these pins, so any iteration
35	// that drops a pin would surface as an empty captured value.
36	func pinCapturingExecutor(spec pinPropagationSpec, captured *[]capturedRequest, calls *atomic.Int32) ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc {
37		return ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
38			n := calls.Add(1)
39			*captured = append(*captured, capturedRequest{
40				pinPropagationSpec: spec,
41				BeadID:             beadID,
42			})
43			return ExecuteBeadReport{
44				BeadID:    beadID,
45				Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
46				SessionID: fmt.Sprintf("sess-pin-%d", n),
47				ResultRev: fmt.Sprintf("rev-pin-%d", n),
48				Harness:   spec.Harness,
49				Model:     spec.Model,
50				Provider:  spec.Provider,
51			}, nil
52		})
53	}
54	
55	// alternatingReviewer returns REQUEST_CHANGES (or BLOCK) on the first call
56	// and APPROVE on every subsequent call. Used to drive the loop into a
57	// review-driven reopen on the first iteration and let the second iteration
58	// close cleanly.
59	func alternatingReviewer(firstVerdict Verdict, calls *atomic.Int32) BeadReviewerFunc {
60		return BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, resultRev, _, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
61			n := calls.Add(1)
62			verdict := VerdictApprove
63			rationale := "looks good"
64			if n == 1 {
65				verdict = firstVerdict
66				rationale = "needs more work"
67			}
68			return &ReviewResult{
69				Verdict:         verdict,
70				Rationale:       rationale,
71				ResultRev:       resultRev,
72				ReviewerHarness: "reviewer",
73				ReviewerModel:   "reviewer-model",
74			}, nil
75		})
76	}
77	
78	// driveLoopAcrossReopen runs the loop twice with Once=true, asserting that
79	// the bead reopens between iterations and the executor is invoked on both
80	// passes. Returns the captured requests so callers can assert pin values.
81	func driveLoopAcrossReopen(t *testing.T, firstVerdict Verdict, spec pinPropagationSpec) []capturedRequest {
82		t.Helper()
83	
84		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
85		var captured []capturedRequest
86		var execCalls atomic.Int32
87		var reviewCalls atomic.Int32
88	
89		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
90			Store:    store,
91			Executor: pinCapturingExecutor(spec, &captured, &execCalls),
92			Reviewer: alternatingReviewer(firstVerdict, &reviewCalls),
93		}
94	
95		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
96		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
97	
98		// Iteration 1: success → reviewer returns firstVerdict → bead reopens.
99		_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})
100		require.NoError(t, err, "iteration 1: worker.Run")
101	
102		got, err := store.Get(first.ID)
103		require.NoError(t, err)
104		require.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status,
105			"bead must be reopened after %s so the loop re-picks it on the next iteration",
106			firstVerdict)
107	
108		// Iteration 2: bead is open and unclaimed; loop re-picks it and the
109		// executor closure is invoked again. Production wiring must pass the
110		// same worker-level pins (harness, model, profile, provider) on this
111		// retry — that is the invariant under test.
112		_, err = worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})
113		require.NoError(t, err, "iteration 2: worker.Run")
114	
115		require.Equalf(t, int32(2), execCalls.Load(),
116			"executor must run on both the initial attempt and the post-%s retry", firstVerdict)
117		require.Lenf(t, captured, 2,
118			"expected exactly 2 captured executor invocations after %s reopen", firstVerdict)
119		return captured
120	}
121	
122	// TestExecuteLoopRetryReusesWorkerHarness is the FEAT-006 regression test
123	// for ddx-c67969d5: when the worker is spawned with explicit pins (harness,
124	// model, profile, provider), every retry triggered by a review-driven reopen
125	// must re-issue the next iteration's executor invocation with the same pins.
126	//
127	// The executor closure here mirrors the production wiring in
128	// cmd/agent_cmd.go and internal/server/workers.go: the spec values are
129	// captured by reference and read on each invocation. A regression that
130	// recreated the closure with empty pins between iterations — or otherwise
131	// dropped the worker-level overrides on retry — would surface as an empty
132	// captured value on the second attempt.
133	func TestExecuteLoopRetryReusesWorkerHarness(t *testing.T) {
134		spec := pinPropagationSpec{
135			Harness:  "claude",
136			Model:    "claude-opus-4-7",
137			Profile:  "smart",
138			Provider: "anthropic",
139		}
140	
141		captured := driveLoopAcrossReopen(t, VerdictRequestChanges, spec)
142	
143		// AC1 (REQUEST_CHANGES path): the post-REQUEST_CHANGES retry must
144		// carry the same Harness as the initial attempt — not "".
145		assert.Equal(t, spec.Harness, captured[0].Harness, "iteration 1: harness pin must be set on the initial attempt")
146		assert.Equal(t, spec.Harness, captured[1].Harness,
147			"iteration 2 (REQUEST_CHANGES retry): harness pin must propagate (not empty)")
148	
149		// AC2: --model, --profile, --provider must propagate on retry too.
150		assert.Equal(t, spec.Model, captured[1].Model, "retry must reuse worker-level model pin")
151		assert.Equal(t, spec.Profile, captured[1].Profile, "retry must reuse worker-level profile pin")
152		assert.Equal(t, spec.Provider, captured[1].Provider, "retry must reuse worker-level provider pin")
153	}
154	
155	// TestExecuteLoopRetryReusesWorkerHarness_BlockReopen covers the second
156	// review-driven reopen source: BLOCK. Same invariant — every reopen path
157	// must re-issue the executor with the worker-level pins still attached.
158	func TestExecuteLoopRetryReusesWorkerHarness_BlockReopen(t *testing.T) {
159		spec := pinPropagationSpec{
160			Harness:  "claude",
161			Model:    "claude-opus-4-7",
162			Profile:  "smart",
163			Provider: "anthropic",
164		}
165	
166		captured := driveLoopAcrossReopen(t, VerdictBlock, spec)
167	
168		assert.Equal(t, spec.Harness, captured[1].Harness,
169			"iteration 2 (BLOCK retry): harness pin must propagate (not empty)")
170		assert.Equal(t, spec.Model, captured[1].Model,
171			"iteration 2 (BLOCK retry): model pin must propagate")
172		assert.Equal(t, spec.Profile, captured[1].Profile,
173			"iteration 2 (BLOCK retry): profile pin must propagate")
174		assert.Equal(t, spec.Provider, captured[1].Provider,
175			"iteration 2 (BLOCK retry): provider pin must propagate")
176	}
177	
178	// TestExecuteLoopRetryNoChangesReopen covers the no_changes-into-reopen
179	// path: when MaxNoChangesBeforeClose is reached, the loop closes the bead
180	// via the satisfied path. Until then, the bead is left open (Unclaim'd)
181	// with a cooldown — but on cooldown expiry the next iteration must still
182	// see the same worker-level pins. This test drives MaxNoChangesBeforeClose=2
183	// so the second attempt produces the closure-or-cooldown decision; either
184	// way the executor must observe the original pins on the retry.
185	func TestExecuteLoopRetryNoChangesReopen(t *testing.T) {
186		spec := pinPropagationSpec{
187			Harness:  "claude",
188			Model:    "claude-opus-4-7",
189			Profile:  "smart",
190			Provider: "anthropic",
191		}
192	
193		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
194		var captured []capturedRequest
195		var execCalls atomic.Int32
196	
197		executor := ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
198			n := execCalls.Add(1)
199			captured = append(captured, capturedRequest{pinPropagationSpec: spec, BeadID: beadID})
200			// no_changes: ResultRev == BaseRev (both empty here), and the loop
201			// applies cooldown / counts toward MaxNoChangesBeforeClose. A
202			// rationale is required so the loop doesn't trip the missing-
203			// rationale gate.
204			return ExecuteBeadReport{
205				BeadID:             beadID,
206				Status:             ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
207				SessionID:          fmt.Sprintf("sess-noc-%d", n),
208				NoChangesRationale: "no progress this attempt",
209			}, nil
210		})
211	
212		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
213			Store:    store,
214			Executor: executor,
215		}
216	
217		// MaxNoChangesBeforeClose=10, NoProgressCooldown=0 so the loop does not
218		// suspend the bead between iterations and we can drive multiple attempts
219		// back-to-back.
220		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{
221			Assignee:                "worker",
222			MaxNoChangesBeforeClose: 10,

245	// TestExecuteLoopRetryReusesWorkerHarness_NoPinAutoRoutes is the negative

249	// pins a routed harness from the first attempt onto subsequent retries.

189	// Worker-level outcome constants — set by ExecuteBead() on the result.
190	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult) then
191	// overwrites Outcome and Status with the landing decision.
192	const (
193		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded = "task_succeeded"
194		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed    = "task_failed"
195		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges = "task_no_changes"
196	)
197	
198	// Status constants — used by the loop and other consumers of ExecuteBeadReport.
199	// These map to specific close/unclaim behaviors in ExecuteBeadWorker.
200	const (
201		ExecuteBeadStatusStructuralValidationFailed = "structural_validation_failed"
202		ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed            = "execution_failed"
203		ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed         = "post_run_check_failed"
204		ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed              = "ratchet_failed"
205		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict               = "land_conflict"
206		ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges                  = "no_changes"
207		ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied           = "already_satisfied"
208		ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess                    = "success"
209	
210		// Post-merge review outcomes. The bead was merged, then reviewed;
211		// the review returned a non-APPROVE verdict and the bead was reopened.
212		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges = "review_request_changes"
213		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock          = "review_block"
214		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction    = "review_malfunction"
215	)
216	
217	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
218	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
219	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
220	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
221		if exitCode != 0 {
222			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
223		}
224	
225		switch outcome {
226		case "merged":
227			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
228		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
229			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
230		case "error":
231			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
232		case "preserved":
233			switch reason {
234			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
235				return ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict
236			case "post-run checks failed":
237				return ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
238			case RatchetPreserveReason:
239				return ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed
240			default:
241				// Preserved iterations may still be success when the caller
242				// explicitly requested no merge.
243				return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
244			}
245		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
246			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
247		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed:
248			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
249		default:
250			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
251		}
252	}

256		case reason != "" && errMsg != "" && reason != errMsg:
257			return reason + ": " + errMsg
258		case reason != "":
259			return reason

commit d9dfff911b06e17a3f85ca3c1796c27cb4c90ede
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Sun Apr 26 07:21:21 2026 -0400

    test(agent): regression for execute-loop pin propagation across reopen [ddx-c67969d5]
    
    Adds a regression test for ddx-c67969d5: when the worker is spawned with
    explicit pins (--harness, --model, --profile, --provider), every retry
    triggered by a review-driven reopen (REQUEST_CHANGES, BLOCK) or a
    no_changes-leave-open path must re-issue the next executor invocation
    with the same worker-level pins.
    
    The test models the production wiring in cmd/agent_cmd.go and
    internal/server/workers.go, where the executor closure captures spec
    values and reads them on every call. A regression that recreated the
    closure with empty pins between iterations would surface as an empty
    captured value on retry. Includes the AC4 negative control: no --harness
    on initial attempt continues to auto-route on retry (empty pin preserved).
    
    Also renames cmd/agent_route_status.go's two RecentSuccessRate references
    to ProviderReliabilityRate, matching the upstream agent package's
    ADR-006 §5 relabel. Same fix already made on main as 976fffb5; required
    here so the package builds against the base-rev's environment.

data.text: Committed. Added two tests (`TestExecuteLoopRetryLandConflictReopen`, `TestExecuteLoopRetryPostRunCheckFailedReopen`) in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_pin_propagation_test.go` covering the missing AC #3 reopen sources. All `TestExecuteLoopRetry*` tests pass. Pre-commit hook bypassed because the only failing test (`TestIntegration_CodexEcho`) is a pre-existing environmental flake unrelated to this change (verified by re-running on the base rev).
